AV.name: Attack Vector
AV.description: This metric reflects the context by which vulnerability exploitation is possible. The lower the value, the more an attacker must be close to the target component.

N.name: Network
N.description: The vulnerable component is bound to the network stack and the attacker's path is through OSI layer 3. Example: sending crafted packets over the internet.

A.name: Adjacent
A.description: The attack is limited to the same shared physical or logical network and cannot be performed across OSI layer 3 boundaries. Example: same LAN, Bluetooth, local wireless.

L.name: Local
L.description: The vulnerable component is not bound to the network stack, and the attacker's path is via read/write/execute capabilities. Example: requires user to execute a malicious file.

P.name: Physical
P.description: The attack requires the attacker to physically touch or manipulate the vulnerable component. Example: inserting a USB device, physical console access.

AC.name: Attack Complexity
AC.description: This metric describes the conditions beyond the attacker's control that must exist in order to exploit the vulnerability. Lower complexity means easier exploitation.

L.name: Low
L.description: Specialized access conditions or extenuating circumstances do not exist. An attacker can expect repeatable success against the vulnerable component.

H.name: High
H.description: A successful attack depends on conditions beyond the attacker's control. Example: specific configuration, winning a race condition.

PR.name: Privileges Required
PR.description: This metric describes the level of privileges an attacker must possess before successfully exploiting the vulnerability.

N.name: None
N.description: The attacker is unauthorized prior to attack, and therefore does not require any access to settings or information of the vulnerable system.

L.name: Low
L.description: The attacker requires privileges that provide basic user capabilities that could normally affect only settings and files owned by a user.

H.name: High
H.description: The attacker requires privileges that provide significant (e.g., administrative) control over the vulnerable component allowing access to component-wide settings and files.

UI.name: User Interaction
UI.description: This metric captures the requirement for a human user, other than the attacker, to participate in the successful compromise of the vulnerable component.

N.name: None
N.description: The vulnerable system can be exploited without interaction from any user.

R.name: Required
R.description: Successful exploitation of this vulnerability requires a user to take some action before the vulnerability can be exploited. Example: clicking a link, opening a file.

P.name: Passive
P.description: Requires passive interaction, such as visiting a web page containing malicious content (CVSS 4.0).

A.name: Active
A.description: Requires active interaction, such as importing a file or confirming a security warning (CVSS 4.0).

S.name: Scope
S.description: Scope identifies whether the vulnerability can affect resources beyond the authorization privileges intended by the vulnerable component.

U.name: Unchanged
U.description: An exploited vulnerability can only affect resources managed by the same security authority. The vulnerable component and impacted component are the same.

C.name: Changed
C.description: An exploited vulnerability can affect resources beyond the authorization privileges intended by the vulnerable component. The vulnerable component and impacted component are different.

C.name: Confidentiality Impact
C.description: This metric measures the impact to the confidentiality of the information resources managed by a software component due to a successfully exploited vulnerability.

H.name: High
H.description: There is total loss of confidentiality, resulting in all resources within the impacted component being divulged to the attacker.

L.name: Low
L.description: Access to some restricted information is obtained, but the attacker does not have control over what information is obtained.

N.name: None
N.description: There is no loss of confidentiality within the impacted component.

I.name: Integrity Impact
I.description: This metric measures the impact to integrity of a successfully exploited vulnerability.

H.name: High
H.description: There is a total loss of integrity, or a complete loss of protection. The attacker can modify any/all files protected by the impacted component.

L.name: Low
L.description: Modification of data is possible, but the attacker does not have control over the consequence of a modification.

N.name: None
N.description: There is no loss of integrity within the impacted component.

A.name: Availability Impact
A.description: This metric measures the impact to the availability of the impacted component resulting from a successfully exploited vulnerability.

H.name: High
H.description: There is total loss of availability, resulting in the attacker being able to fully deny access to resources in the impacted component.

L.name: Low
L.description: There is reduced performance or interruptions in resource availability.

N.name: None
N.description: There is no impact to availability within the impacted component.

AT.name: Attack Requirements
AT.description: This metric captures the prerequisite deployment and execution conditions or variables of the vulnerable system that enable the attack (CVSS 4.0).

N.name: None
N.description: There are no specific deployment or execution conditions required for the attack to succeed.

P.name: Present
P.description: The attack relies on specific deployment or execution conditions, such as winning a race condition or network injection (MITM).

VC.name: Vulnerable System Confidentiality
VC.description: Measures the impact to the confidentiality of the vulnerable system (CVSS 4.0).

H.name: High
H.description: Total or sensitive information disclosure.

VI.name: Vulnerable System Integrity
VI.description: Measures the impact to the integrity of the vulnerable system (CVSS 4.0).

H.name: High
H.description: Arbitrary file modification or severe tampering.

VA.name: Vulnerable System Availability
VA.description: Measures the impact to the availability of the vulnerable system (CVSS 4.0).

H.name: High
H.description: Total denial of service.

SC.name: Subsequent System Confidentiality
SC.description: Measures the impact to the confidentiality of subsequent systems, replacing Scope in CVSS 3.x (CVSS 4.0).

H.name: High
H.description: Sensitive information disclosed to subsequent systems.

L.name: Low
L.description: Limited information disclosed to subsequent systems.

SI.name: Subsequent System Integrity
SI.description: Measures the impact to the integrity of subsequent systems (CVSS 4.0).

H.name: High
H.description: Complete loss of integrity in subsequent systems.

SA.name: Subsequent System Availability
SA.description: Measures the impact to the availability of subsequent systems (CVSS 4.0).

H.name: High
H.description: Total denial of service in subsequent systems.

E.name: Exploit Code Maturity
E.description: This metric measures the likelihood of the vulnerability being attacked, and is typically based on the current state of exploit techniques, exploit code availability, or active, 'in-the-wild' use.

X.name: Not Defined
X.description: Does not change the score, uses default value.

H.name: High
H.description: Functional autonomous code exists, or no exploit is required. Exploit development has reached the level of reliable, widely-available, easy-to-use automated tools.

F.name: Functional
F.description: Functional exploit code is available. The code works in most situations where the vulnerability exists.

P.name: Proof-of-Concept
P.description: Proof-of-concept exploit code is available, or an attack demonstration is not practical for most systems.

U.name: Unproven
U.description: No exploit code is available, or an exploit is theoretical.

A.name: Attacked
A.description: Attacks have been reported or exploit tools are publicly available (CVSS 4.0).

RL.name: Remediation Level
RL.description: This metric describes the level of remediation available for the vulnerability.

U.name: Unavailable
U.description: There is either no solution available or it is impossible to apply.

W.name: Workaround
W.description: There is an unofficial, non-vendor solution available. This includes situations where the community has developed a solution.

T.name: Temporary Fix
T.description: There is an official but temporary fix available. This includes instances where the vendor issues a temporary hotfix or patch.

O.name: Official Fix
O.description: A complete vendor solution is available. The vendor has issued an official patch, or the vulnerability is fixed in a newer version.

RC.name: Report Confidence
RC.description: This metric measures the degree of confidence in the existence of the vulnerability and the credibility of the known technical details.

C.name: Confirmed
C.description: Detailed reports exist, or functional reproduction is possible. The author or vendor of the affected code has confirmed the presence of the vulnerability.

R.name: Reasonable
R.description: Significant details are published, but researchers either do not have full confidence in the root cause, or do not have access to source code.

U.name: Unknown
U.description: There are reports of impacts that indicate a vulnerability is present. The reports indicate that the cause of the vulnerability is unknown.

CR.name: Confidentiality Requirement
CR.description: This metric enables the analyst to specify the importance of the confidentiality of the information to the organization.

H.name: High
H.description: Loss of confidentiality is likely to have a catastrophic adverse effect on the organization.

M.name: Medium
M.description: Loss of confidentiality is likely to have a serious adverse effect on the organization.

L.name: Low
L.description: Loss of confidentiality is likely to have only a limited adverse effect on the organization.

IR.name: Integrity Requirement
IR.description: This metric enables the analyst to specify the importance of the integrity of the information to the organization.

H.name: High
H.description: Loss of integrity is likely to have a catastrophic adverse effect on the organization.

M.name: Medium
M.description: Loss of integrity is likely to have a serious adverse effect on the organization.

L.name: Low
L.description: Loss of integrity is likely to have only a limited adverse effect on the organization.

AR.name: Availability Requirement
AR.description: This metric enables the analyst to specify the importance of the availability of the information to the organization.

H.name: High
H.description: Loss of availability is likely to have a catastrophic adverse effect on the organization.

M.name: Medium
M.description: Loss of availability is likely to have a serious adverse effect on the organization.

L.name: Low
L.description: Loss of availability is likely to have only a limited adverse effect on the organization.

MAV.name: Modified Attack Vector
MAV.description: Allows overriding the base Attack Vector value.

MAC.name: Modified Attack Complexity
MAC.description: Allows overriding the base Attack Complexity value.

H.name: High
H.description: High complexity.

MAT.name: Modified Attack Requirements
MAT.description: Allows overriding the base Attack Requirements value (CVSS 4.0).

MPR.name: Modified Privileges Required
MPR.description: Allows overriding the base Privileges Required value.

H.name: High
H.description: High privileges.

MUI.name: Modified User Interaction
MUI.description: Allows overriding the base User Interaction value.

MS.name: Modified Scope
MS.description: Allows overriding the base Scope value.

MC.name: Modified Confidentiality
MC.description: Allows overriding the base Confidentiality Impact value.

H.name: High
H.description: High impact.

MI.name: Modified Integrity
MI.description: Allows overriding the base Integrity Impact value.

H.name: High
H.description: High impact.

MA.name: Modified Availability
MA.description: Allows overriding the base Availability Impact value.

H.name: High
H.description: High impact.

MVC.name: Modified Vulnerable System Confidentiality
MVC.description: Allows overriding the Vulnerable System Confidentiality value (CVSS 4.0).

H.name: High
H.description: High impact.

MVI.name: Modified Vulnerable System Integrity
MVI.description: Allows overriding the Vulnerable System Integrity value (CVSS 4.0).

H.name: High
H.description: High impact.

MVA.name: Modified Vulnerable System Availability
MVA.description: Allows overriding the Vulnerable System Availability value (CVSS 4.0).

H.name: High
H.description: High impact.

MSC.name: Modified Subsequent System Confidentiality
MSC.description: Allows overriding the Subsequent System Confidentiality value (CVSS 4.0).

H.name: High
H.description: High impact.

MSI.name: Modified Subsequent System Integrity
MSI.description: Allows overriding the Subsequent System Integrity value (CVSS 4.0).

H.name: High
H.description: High impact.

MSA.name: Modified Subsequent System Availability
MSA.description: Allows overriding the Subsequent System Availability value (CVSS 4.0).

H.name: High
H.description: High impact.

SAF.name: Safety
SAF.description: Measures the potential impact to human safety if the vulnerability were to be exploited (CVSS 4.0).

P.name: Present
P.description: May result in Marginal, Critical, or Catastrophic harm.

AU.name: Automatable
AU.description: Measures whether the vulnerability can be exploited automatically (CVSS 4.0).

Y.name: Yes
Y.description: Can be fully automated, such as worm-type vulnerabilities.

N.name: No
N.description: Cannot automate all 4 steps of the kill chain.

U.name: Provider Urgency
U.description: The vendor's assessment of the urgency of the vulnerability (CVSS 4.0).

R.name: Recovery
R.description: The system's ability to recover from an attack (CVSS 4.0).

U.name: User
U.description: Requires user manual intervention to recover.

V.name: Value Density
V.description: The value density of the affected resources (CVSS 4.0).

D.name: Diffuse
D.description: Limited resources, such as a single email client.

C.name: Concentrated
C.description: Rich resources, such as a central email server.

RE.name: Vulnerability Response Effort
RE.description: The effort required to respond to the vulnerability (CVSS 4.0).

L.name: Low
L.description: Low effort, such as configuration workarounds.

M.name: Moderate
M.description: Moderate effort, such as simple remote updates.

H.name: High
H.description: High effort, such as physical replacement or BIOS updates.
